sn56.28:1.1: “Monks, there are these four noble truths.
sn56.28:1.3: The noble truth of stress, the noble truth of the origination of stress, the noble truth of the cessation of stress, & the noble truth of the path of practice leading to the cessation of stress.
sn56.28:1.4: “In this cosmos with its devas, Māras, & Brahmās, in this generation with its contemplatives & brahmans, its royalty & commonfolk, the Tathāgata is the noble one.
sn56.28:1.5: That is why they are called ‘noble truths.’
sn56.28:2.1: “Therefore, monks, your duty is the contemplation, ‘This is stress …
